We are happy to announce a suite of new features which come directly from your ideas and suggestions around faster app builds, and a more user friendly experience! Please join me in evangelizing the following six (6) top feature releases this month! This new app sample was inspired by someone who posted a Forum Idea regarding PowerApp Themes. We really enjoy reading all the forum ideas, and I refer to those who post ideas as our enthusiastic “innovators”. This particular innovator came up with the idea that we should expose our “Themes” (see screen shot below) to the consumer of the app (aka the person running the app), and not only to the designer (or app maker). What an awesome idea! It really shows that they appreciate Empathic Design. Empathic Design is a process that one of my past managers (Karuana Gatimu) has instilled in me. It is a user-centered design approach that pays attention to the user's feelings toward a product. As I thought about this further, I realized that this could also be even more relevant in mobile app design because factors around mobility can affect the way we format the screen; low light, screen glare, etc. depending on where the person is when they are running the app. It also could apply to accessibility, especially for people like me who always wants to make the font size bigger! texts.full_text: of switching pulses of opposite polarity results in qualitatively different domain switching dynamics. A feature of domain dynamics is roughening of the domains walls during switching to the preferred polarization state as opposed to smooth domain boundaries during switching to the opposite direction. The observed switching behavior is explained by a combinedeffect of disorder potential and intrinsic material properties on ferroelectric domain evolution. The presence of structural defects, compositional inhomogeneities, or interface roughness can create local variations in electric field distribution within the film. These heterogeneities act as pinning sites for domains walls during switching processes, leading to a more complex dynamics compared with defect-free materials. In PVDF-TrFE copolymer films that have been strongly imprinted (i.e., pre-polarized or subjected to conditions favoring one polarization state), these disorder potentials can significantly influence the path and speed of domain wall motion. When an electric field is applied in a direction opposite to the preferred orientation, domains switch more readily due to lower energy barriers for nucleation at defect sites. This results in smoother progression of new boundaries as they form along paths with least resistance from pinning centers or other obstacles within the material matrix. Conversely, switching towards regions already stabilized by imprinting requires overcoming stronger local potentials associated not only with defects but also pre-existing polarization configurations. Thus, during reverse-polarity pulse application: - Domain walls exhibit rougher morphology due to encountering multiple barriers. - Switched domains may form smaller sizes and more irregular shapes as they navigate through a landscape of varying potential wells created by the disorder plus imprinting effects. texts.full_text: and 27 August. She returned to Pearl Harbor on 5 September. Patrolling New Guinea waters After routine overhaul, Tuna set out from Pearl Harbor on 9 November. She made only one contact during her fourth war patrol, firing two torpedoes at a Japanese destroyer operating off New Georgia Island on 12 DeceMber. Both missed their mark. Three days after Christmas 1942, Tuna arrived at her new base, Brisbane, Australia. Setting out again on 18 January 1943 to begin patrol number five, she arrived in waters off the east coast of Vella LaVella six days later. Expending 16 torpedoes in five futile daylight attacks, Tuna pressed home determined forays only to be driven deep by intense enemy antisubmarine countermeasures. Kept on station between New Ireland and Buka after 11 February, Tuna launched further attacks – but from excessive ranges – which only resulted in still more frustration for her crew. Dispatched next to interdict traffic from reinforcing Vila Plantation and Munda, Tuna remained luckless and was forced deep and endured depth-charge attacks before ending the patrol on 7 February. texts.full_text: . The present study was performed in order to further understand the metabolomic changes of ischemia/reperfusion (I/R)-induced AKI and the protective effect of L-carnitine on AKI. Kidney tissues and serum samples were collected at different time points from three groups of rats including control group, I/R group and L-carnitine-pretreated group. High-performance liquid chromatography coupled with mass spectrometry-based metabolomics approach was applied to investigate the characteristic of I/R-induced AKI and the protective effects of L-carnitine in rat kidney I/R model. Antioxidant enzymatic activity and phospholipid changes were also evaluated. Metabolomic profiling revealed significant alterations in several metabolic pathways following ischemia/reperfusion injury, including glycolysis, lipid metabolism, and amino acid metabolism. To create a test, co-locate your test with the file it's testing as `<filename>.test.js` and then run/watch tests with: ```bash yarn test ``` ### Adding new functions/files to Webpack When you add a new function to your serverless config, you don't need to also add it as a new entry for Webpack. His Take on Spring Cleaning . . . No, I don’t usually participate in spring cleaning. I usually wash cotton shirts and jeans. I enjoy the fresh and clean scent of Downy Unstopables while wearing my clothes. This is a great product for those who want to keep their laundry smelling clean, even after multiple wears. I have tried several other detergents in recent years but found that none seemed as effective at keeping the freshness and original fabric softener-like quality. I've noticed an improvement with Downy Unstopables' ability not only wash out stains effectively (which is always a plus) - it also leaves clothes feeling very comfortable to wear, almost like new. Another aspect of this product that caught my attention was its packaging design; sleek and minimalistic yet easy-to-read labels. The eco-friendly bottle has made me more conscious about how I use products in general but Downy Unstopables is definitely one exception as it's not only effective for tough stains, dirt & grease on clothes - especially important during spring cleaning when we are washing winter clothing before summer attire takes over. Overall the product leaves a positive impression and has made me want to continue using this detergent in my laundry routine. It also addressed potential investments that could contribute significantly towards economic development for people living there with a focus particularly at fostering employment opportunities. During the session "Arctic Boost - Partnerships and Programmes", participants were invited by Mr Dagmar Zullich from the Joint Secretariat to present their projects, followed up on in an interactive discussion. The presentation of the Arctic Investment Protocol was one highlight among many others which showcased how a partnership between public bodies at different levels can contribute towards economic development. Native to eastern North America, it has become a common weed along roadsides, fencerows, and even urban landscapes due to its ability to quickly spread through seed dispersal by birds and small mammals. texts.full_text: management of heart failure with the use of therapies such as beta-blockers, ACE inhibitors, aldosterone inhibitors and device therapies that prolong survival after ischaemic heart damage or heart failure related to hypertension and valvular heart disease. There has been limited documentation of the health system impacts of heart failure in the Australian community. Morbidity estimates have typically been derived by applying international chronic heart failure incidence rates to the Australian population, which may not accurately reflect local conditions due to differences in demographics, healthcare systems, and prevalence of risk factors \[[@B4]-[@B6]\]. Consequently, there is a need for more precise data on the burden of heart failure within Australia. ### Objectives This study aims to provide an updated assessment of the health system impacts of heart failure in Australia. Specifically, it seeks to: 1. Estimate the incidence and prevalence of heart failure in the Australian population. 2. Quantify hospital admissions, readmissions, and associated costs for patients with heart failure. 3. Evaluate trends over time in morbidity and mortality related to heart failure. 4. Identify key factors contributing to variations in health outcomes among different subpopulations. ### Methods #### Data Sources Data will be obtained from multiple sources including: - National Hospital Morbidity Database (NHMD): Provides detailed information on hospital admissions, length of stay, diagnoses, and procedures for all public and private hospitals. - Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) mortality data: Offers comprehensive records of deaths by cause, age, sex, and other demographic factors. - Medicare Benefits Schedule (MBS) and Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme (PBS): Data on healthcare services utilization and prescription medications. #### Study Design A retrospective cohort study design will be employed to analyze trends over a specified period. The primary outcomes include hospitalization rates, readmission rates within 30 days of discharge, and total health care costs associated with heart failure management. ### Statistical Analysis - Descriptive Statistics: Incidence and prevalence rates will be calculated using population estimates from the ABS. - Trend A

Many critics have praised Generations of Winter as a new Doctor Zhivago-style, large-scale Russian novel, which tells the story of a Russian/Georgian family, the Gradovs, struggling to survive in the Stalinist era. As the Wall Street Journal put it: "Aksyonov has ambitiously set out to challenge Tolstoy and Pasternak by writing an epic saga that encompasses both personal drama and historical upheaval. Generations of Winter spans three decades, from 1925 through World War II, focusing on the Gradov family’s trials during one of Russia's most tumultuous periods." The novel is divided into several parts, each covering a significant phase in Russian history: the early years under Stalin, collectivization and purges, followed by the Great Patriotic War (World War II). Aksyonov weaves together intimate personal stories with broader historical events, creating a vivid tapestry of life during these times. The Gradovs' story is one of resilience, love, betrayal, and survival against overwhelming odds. Critics have also noted that "Generations of Winter" offers a nuanced portrayal of the Soviet system from within, providing insights into how ordinary people navigated their lives under an oppressive regime while maintaining their humanity. This novel stands as a significant contribution

texts.full_text: f contact failure are grossly classified into three. The first cause is that a conductive film forming a wiring electrode and a source/drain region (semiconductor film) or a lead out electrode (conductive film) are not brought into ohmic contact with each other. This is due to the fact that an insulating coating, for example, a metal oxide or the like is formed on a contact face. The second cause is that the coverage of a conductive film forming a wiring electrode is poor and disconnection is caused in a contact hole. In this case, improvement must be achieved by film forming method or film forming condition of the wiring electrode. Further, the third cause is disconnection of a wiring electrode caused by the sectional shape of a contact hole or the like. The sectional shape of a contact hole is strongly dependent on etching conditions of an insulating material (SiN, SiO2, organic resin film or the like) covered on the contact portion. Particularly, contact failure due to the second or the third cause is actualized with a higher aspect ratio of a contact hole by miniaturizing a semiconductor device. It is an object of the present invention disclosed in the specification to reduce operational failure of a semiconductor device caused by contact failure by resolving the above-described problem. Particularly, it is an object of the present invention to provide the technology of eliminating contact failure when a material comprising aluminum or including aluminum as a major component is used as a wiring electrode. Further, it is an object of the present invention to provide the technology of realizing a semiconductor device or an electro-optic device having high long period reliability by improving reliability of contact. texts.full_text: a bZip Maf transcription factor protein that in humans is encoded by the MAFK gene. MafK is one of the small Maf proteins, which are basic region and leucine zipper (bZIP)-type transcription factors. The HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee-approved gene name of MAFK is “v-maf avian musculoaponeurotic fibrosarcoma oncogene homolog K”. Discovery MafK was first cloned and identified in chicken in 1993 as a member of the small Maf (sMaf) genes. MafK was also identified as p18 NF-E2, a component of NF-E2 complex binding to a specific motif (NF-E2) in the regulatory regions of β-globin and erythroid-related genes. MAFK has been identified in many vertebrates, including humans. There are three functionally redundant sMaf proteins in vertebrates, MafF, MafG, and MafK. Structure MafK has a bZIP structure that consists of a basic region for DNA binding and a leucine zipper structure for dimer formation. Similar to other sMafs, MafK lacks any canonical transcriptional activation domains. Expression MAFK is broadly but differentially expressed in various tissues. MAFK expression was detected in all 16 tissues examinedHere is the completed text based on the information provided: MAFK is a bZip Maf transcription factor protein that is encoded by the MAFK gene in humans. It is one of the small Maf proteins, which are basic region and leucine zipper (bZIP)-type transcription factors. MAFK was first cloned and identified in chicken in 1993 and has since been identified in many vertebrates, including humans. It has a bZIP structure that consists of a basic region for DNA binding and a leucine zipper structure for dimer formation, and it lacks any canonical transcriptional activation domains. MAFK is broadly but differentially expressed in various tissues.

The good news is that I have just published an ebook based on this blog. It’s called Mama Insider: Laughing (And Sometimes Crying) All the Way Through Pregnancy, Birth, and the First 3 Months. It’s a collection of 20+ of my best posts, plus some new material, all in one pretty little e-package. For just $4.99, you can download the PDF version here or get the e-reader version here. Mama Insider will give you the real scoop on pregnancy, birth, and new motherhood – the good, the bad, and the funny – from one (formerly) new mom to another. With millions of copies sold worldwide in multiple languages, it is the style manual of choice for writers, researchers, editors, students, and educators in the social and behavioral sciences, natural sciences, nursing, communications, education, business, engineeRing, and other fields. Known for itscomprehensive coverage on grammar rules with real-world examples. It offers guidance to help improve your writing skills while ensuring that it meets professional standards of clarity and conciseness. The Publication Manual has been updated throughout the years since first published in 1929 by APA (American Psychological Association). The seventh edition is a must-have for anyone looking to write with authority on scientific findings. It covers all aspects, from manuscript preparation through publication as well covering citation standards that are essential when referencing other people's work. This manual provides guidelines and examples of how different elements should be formatted including in-text citations (e.g., Smith & Jones 2018), reference lists at the end of your document or paper with both print sources such a journal article, book chapter etc. as well electronic ones like web pages online databases). Whether you are an undergraduate student writing their first research report for class assignment; doctoral candidate preparing to submit work on which they hope receive publication credit and recognition within field academic circles – having this manual at hand will be invaluable asset throughout entire process from initial conception right through final submission. texts.full_text: 2, we sought to assess their overall survival, local control rate, and functional rehabilitation outcomes. The patient population consisted of individuals aged between 35 and 73 years, all diagnosed with squamous cell carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. Treatment modalities included SL or ESL, along with ipsilateral or bilateral neck dissections, and in some cases, postoperative radiotherapy. All patients were staged according to the 1997 UICC TNM classification system. To measure survival, we employed the Kaplan-Meyer method, which revealed an overall survival rate of 77% and a local control rate of 78%. These figures indicate that a significant majority of the patients experienced long-term survival following the procedure, with a high percentage maintaining local control over their disease. Functional rehabilitation was also assessed in this study. The mean time for nasogastric tube removal post-surgery was found to be 16 days (ranging from 10 to 39 days) for SL cases and 23 days (ranging from 15 to 50 days) for ESL cases. This information is crucial as it provides insights into the recovery timeline, which can be used by healthcare professionals to better manage patient care and expectations post-surgery. Regarding complications, the overall incidence was reported at 20%. The most frequently encountered issues included postoperative infection and bleeding. Understanding these common complications allows for proactive measures to be taken during and after surgery to mitigate their occurrence and potential impact on patient outcomes. In conclusion, this follow-up study of 142 patients treated with Marullo's supraglottic laryngectomy technique between 1976 and 1992 provides valuable data on the long-term outcomes of this surgical approach. The high overall survival and local control rates underscore the effectiveness of this procedure in managing squamous cell carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. Additionally, insights into functional rehabilitation timelines and common complications enhance our understanding of how to optimize patient care following SL

texts.full_text: a diagnosis of chronic thromboembolic disease (CTED). The patient was refractory to anticoagulation therapy over the following weeks without significant improvement in symptoms or objective measures. This prompted further investigation and re-evaluation. Upon closer review, it became evident that while some features on imaging were suggestive of CTED—such as central pulmonary artery dilatation with peripheral pruning—a detailed clinical correlation raised suspicion for an alternative diagnosis given her systemic manifestations (oral ulcers, rash) which are not typical in chronic thromboembolic disease. A high index of suspicion led to the consideration and eventual confirmation through biopsy that she had a form of vasculitis affecting pulmonary vessels. The specific type was determined after histopathological examination—revealing granulomatous inflammation with multinucleated giant cells, pointing towards Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis (GPA), previously known as Wegener'sGranulomatosis. texts.full_text: Dakota, Respondent and Appellee. No. 14968. Supreme Court of South Dakota. Considered on Briefs January 15, 1986. Decided December 23, 1986. *141 Cynthia A. howard, Northern Hills Public Defender's Office, Deadwood, for petitioner and appellant. Robert Mayer, Asst. Atty. Gen., Pierre, for respOndent and appellee; Mark V. Meierhenry, Atty. Gen., Pierre, on brief. MORGAN, Justice. This is an appeal from an order quashing a writ of habeas corpus originally issued on the petition of Gary Loop (Loop) which alleged, among other things, an unconstitutional deprivation of effective assistance of appellate counsel. We reverse and remand. On November 4, 1981, Loop was charged with the offense of sexual contact with a child under fifteen yFacts: Gary Lynn Loop ("Loop") appealed from an order quashing his writ of habeas corpus filed by him as petitioner-appellant against Herman Solem ("Solem"), Warden at South Dakota State Penitentiary, who was respondent-appellee. The appeal challenged the constitutionality of deprivation and denial of effective assistance of appellate counsel. Loop's conviction for sexual contact with a child under fifteen years old in November 1981 resulted from his trial on January 25-30, 1984 ( Loop v. State , SD., No. CR79-67). The Court of Appeals affirmed the conviction and sentence on May 16, 1985. Loop sought habeas corpus relief in June 1985 based primarily upon ineffective assistance claims against his appellate counsel from the Office of Attorney General (OAG), which was appointed to represent him during post-conviction proceedings. The Circuit Court denied Loop's petition and quashed the writ on November 26, 1985. Issue: WhetherLoop received effective assistance of appellate counsel under Strickland v.Washington , U.S., --- S.Ct. [---]
